CGAL::Skin_surface_3.name: CGAL::Skin_surface_3
CGAL::Skin_surface_3.brief: The Skin_surface_3 is the main class in this package.
CGAL::Skin_surface_3.detailed: It is a model of the concept SkinSurface_3 . The template argument must be a model of the concept SkinSurfaceTraits_3 , which means that it provides predicates to construct a regular triangulation of the weighted points and for point location in the mixed complex. Is model of SkinSurface_3

CGAL::Skin_surface_polyhedral_items_3.name: CGAL::Skin_surface_polyhedral_items_3
CGAL::Skin_surface_polyhedral_items_3.brief: An items class for the Polyhedron_3 that stores information in faces instead of an auxiliary data structure.
CGAL::Skin_surface_polyhedral_items_3.detailed: Using it accelerates the subdivision function subdivide_skin_surface_mesh_3() .

CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3.name: CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3
CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3.brief: The class Skin_surface_traits_3 is designed as a default traits class for the class Skin_surface_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 > .
CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3.detailed: It is a model of the SkinSurfaceTraits_3 . K must be a model of the Kernel concept. Is model of SkinSurfaceTraits_3

CGAL::Union_of_balls_3.name: CGAL::Union_of_balls_3
CGAL::Union_of_balls_3.brief: The Union_of_balls_3 is used to represent a skin surface with shrink factor equal to one, which is the boundary of the union of the input balls.
CGAL::Union_of_balls_3.detailed: This case is handled separately since the mixed complex is equal to the power diagram, which has a much simpler structure. The template argument must be a model of the concept SkinSurfaceTraits_3 , which means that it provides the predicates to construct a regular triangulation of the weighted points. Is model of SkinSurface_3

SkinSurface_3.name: SkinSurface_3
SkinSurface_3.brief: The concept SkinSurface_3 defines a skin surface and provides an interface for the dedicated skin surface mesher. The concept requires a constructor from an iterator range of weighted points and a shrink factor. By default the input balls are grown in such that the skin surface wraps around the input balls.
SkinSurface_3.detailed: Has models CGAL::Skin_surface_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 > CGAL::Union_of_balls_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 >

members.name: Geometric_traits
members.brief: The geometric traits used for the construction of the regular triangulation.

members.name: FT
members.brief: The FT type defined by the Geometric_traits .
members.detailed: This is the number type used by the weighted points.

members.name: Skin_surface_3
members.brief: The mandatory arguments to the constructor are an iterator range [begin,end) of weighted points and a shrink factor between 0 and 1.

members.name: mesh_skin_surface_3
members.brief: Constructs a coarse mesh in p .
members.detailed: Polyhedron must be an instance of Polyhedron_3 . Polyhedron::HDS can be used as the template argument of the CGAL::Polyhedron_incremental_builder_3<HDS> .

members.name: subdivide_skin_surface_mesh_3
members.brief: Subdivides the skin surface using nSubdiv 1-4 split operations (each triangle is split into four sub-triangles) and the new vertices are moved towards the skin surface.
members.detailed: Polyhedron must be an instance of Polyhedron_3 .

SkinSurfaceTraits_3.name: SkinSurfaceTraits_3
SkinSurfaceTraits_3.brief: Required types and member functions for the SkinSurfaceTraits_3 concept. This geometric traits concept is used for the construction of a polyhedral mesh approximating a skin surface CGAL::Skin_surface_3 .
SkinSurfaceTraits_3.detailed: Refines RegularTriangulationTraits_3 Has models CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3 <K> CGAL::Skin_surface_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 > CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3 <K>

members.name: Construct_anchor_point_3
members.brief: A constructor object which provides the following function operator:
members.detailed: Point operator()(const Point &center_del, const Point &center_vor); Constructs the anchor point in between the Delaunay and Voronoi centers, i.e., the point $ (1-s)\cdot$ center_del + $ s\cdot$ center_vor , where $ s$ is the shrink factor.

members.name: SkinSurfaceTraits_3
members.brief: Constructor that takes the shrink factor as argument.
members.detailed: For meshing the boundary of the union of a set of balls, the shrink factor is discarded.

PkgSkinSurface3Ref.name: PkgSkinSurface3Ref
PkgSkinSurface3Ref.detailed: Nico Kruithof This package allows to build a triangular mesh of a skin surface. Skin surfaces are used for modeling large molecules in biological computing. The surface is defined by a set of balls, representing the atoms of the molecule, and a shrink factor that determines the size of the smooth patches gluing the balls together. The construction of a triangular mesh of a smooth skin surface is often necessary for further analysis and for fast visualization. This package provides functions to construct a triangular mesh approximating the skin surface from a set of balls and a shrink factor. It also contains code to subdivide the mesh efficiently. Introduced in: CGAL 3.3 Depends on: PkgTriangulation3 and PkgPolyhedron BibTeX: cgal:k-ssm3-26a License: GPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts SkinSurface_3 SkinSurfaceTraits_3 Classes CGAL::Skin_surface_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 > CGAL::Union_of_balls_3 < SkinSurfaceTraits_3 > CGAL::Skin_surface_traits_3 <K> Functions CGAL::make_skin_surface_mesh_3 <Polyhedron_3>() CGAL::mesh_skin_surface_3 < SkinSurface_3 , Polyhedron_3>() CGAL::subdivide_skin_surface_mesh_3 < SkinSurface_3 , Polyhedron_3>()

members.name: make_skin_surface_mesh_3
members.brief: constructs a mesh of the skin surface defined by the weighted points and the shrink factor.
members.detailed: The function make_skin_surface_mesh_3() constructs a mesh isotopic to the skin surface based on the algorithm in [2] . It takes as input a range of weighted points and a shrink factor and outputs the mesh in a Polyhedron_3 object. A number of subdivision steps might be applied to refine the mesh. WP_iterator must be an input iterator with weighted points as value type. Polyhedron must be an instance of Polyhedron_3 . Polyhedron::HDS can be used as the template argument of the Polyhedron_incremental_builder_3<HDS> .
members.argsstring: (Polyhedron &p, WP_iterator begin, WP_iterator end, double shrink_factor=.5, int nSubdivisions=0, bool grow_balls=true)

members.name: mesh_skin_surface_3
members.brief: constructs a mesh of the skin_surface in p .
members.detailed: The function mesh_skin_surface_3() constructs a mesh isotopic to the skin surface based on the algorithm in [2] . It takes as input a SkinSurface_3 object, which is a model of the SkinSurface_3 concept and outputs the mesh in a Polyhedron_3 object. SkinSurface_3 must be a model of the concept SkinSurface_3 . Polyhedron must be an instance of Polyhedron_3 . Polyhedron::HDS can be used as the template argument of the Polyhedron_incremental_builder_3<HDS> .

members.name: subdivide_skin_surface_mesh_3
members.brief: subdivides a skin surface mesh constructed by the function mesh_skin_surface_3() using nSubdiv 1-4 split operations (each triangle is split into four sub-triangles) and the new vertices are moved towards the skin surface.
members.detailed: SkinSurface_3 must be a model of the concept SkinSurface_3 . Polyhedron must be an instance of Polyhedron_3 .
